settings.title: Settings
engines.title: Engine routing
engines.help: Which service handles each part of the pipeline. Endpoints are machine-level settings; dots show live reachability.
engines.remoteColdStart: Speaks the MACU render-service API (:8779). First render after the service starts ≈ 70 s (kernel compile).
engines.unreachable: unreachable — start the service or check the URL
engines.envOverrideTip: Pinned by an environment variable (.env / systemd) — saved values are ignored while it's set.
settings.hf.title: Higgsfield.ai account
settings.hf.help: Connect your Higgsfield.ai account to generate video shots, character stills, and lip-synced clips in the cloud. Uses your Higgsfield subscription plan and credits.
settings.hf.openedTab: A Higgsfield sign-in tab was opened.
settings.hf.pasteToggle: Trouble connecting? Paste the redirect URL
settings.hf.pasteHelp: If the sign-in page ends on an unreachable page after you approve, copy that page's full URL from the address bar and paste it here.
settings.hf.modelsHelp: Models available to your account. Pick one per shot in Video → Add Shot (kind: higgsfield / lipsync). 👄 = accepts an audio track (usable for lipsync).
settings.hf.modelsFailed: could not load the model catalog
settings.hf.lipsyncCapable: Accepts an audio track — usable for lipsync shots
settings.lang.title: Language
settings.lang.help: Changes the interface language. Applies instantly and is remembered on this device.
settings.theme.title: Color theme
settings.theme.help: Recolors the primary accent. Applies instantly and is remembered on this device.
settings.git.stub: Configure the remote URL and branch the Studio syncs script/manifest/youtube to. Credentials use the box's existing SSH key. Coming soon.
settings.show.name: Display name
tour.projectMenu.title: Project menu
tour.projectMenu.body: New or open shows and episodes, import/export a project, and settings — all live here. Studio can hold multiple shows now.
tour.episodePicker.title: Episode picker
tour.episodePicker.body: Pick an episode; the ◀ ▶ arrows step through them. The dot is its git-sync state — green means pushed, red means local changes.
tour.script.title: Script
tour.script.body: Write the episode in Markdown, then hit Generate manifest to turn it into cues the pipeline can render.
tour.audio.title: Audio
tour.audio.body: Per-cue voiceover — play it, regenerate a line, and drop sound effects into the gaps between cues.
tour.graphics.title: Graphics
tour.graphics.body: Title cards and on-screen overlays. Generate card text and render the compositions that get composited over the video.
tour.video.title: Video
tour.video.body: The shot list — every character and b-roll clip. Render the missing or stale ones; the preview shows each result.
tour.assembly.title: Assembly
tour.assembly.body: The render dashboard. Runs the full pipeline end-to-end to a finished video, with live per-stage progress. It's the deep end — explore it once the rest clicks.
tour.publish.title: Publish
tour.publish.body: The last stop: finalize the episode for the web — title, description, thumbnail, the hosted video link, and who can see it — then Publish to push it to mayorawesome.com.
tour.docs.title: Docs
tour.docs.body: The canon — character bible, prompt docs, and pipeline notes. Reference, not editing.
tour.gitSync.title: Git sync
tour.gitSync.body: Commits this episode's text — script, manifest, youtube.txt — into the repo and pushes it. Generated media stays local; this is the portable source of truth.
tour.mcp.title: Bring your own agent
tour.mcp.body: Studio is also an MCP server — everything in this tour is a tool an AI agent can call. Point Claude Code or Claude Desktop at http://localhost:8774/mcp and the workflow becomes one conversation: write the script, cast the voices, render, publish. See the Connect-an-agent docs for setup.
tour.ready.title: Ready to start
tour.ready.body: You're all set — begin by creating a new show from scratch: open the project menu and pick New show…. Reopen this tour anytime from the same menu → Tutorial. Have fun.
assembly.noEvents: (no events yet — click a Run button)
assembly.noSrt: No SRT yet. Run stage 7 first.
localize.title: Localize — subtitles + dub
localize.renderFirst: Render this episode in English first — localization reuses the rendered picture and subtitles, so it needs a finished render.
localize.haikuNote: For the sharpest comedy translation, ask Claude in the terminal drawer — there's no cloud hook in the app.
localize.runningNote: Each language renders on the GPU queue, one at a time. You can close this — it keeps going.
localize.busyOrUnrendered: render the episode first, or wait for the active render to finish
asset.allEpsTitle: Shots / VO / cards: show assets from EVERY episode (not just this one). SFX & music are always shared.
asset.altsTitle: SHOTS tab: also list the non-live takes (earlier generations) of each shot. Drag one in to use that take.
asset.shotDrag: drag {key} onto a cue in the SHOTS track
asset.shotDragForeign: drag {key} onto a cue in the SHOTS track (imports from {slug})
asset.altDrag: {key} — take v{v} · drag onto a cue to use this take
asset.noShots: No shots — add them on the Video tab.
asset.cardDragForeign: drag {key} onto the GRAPHICS track (imports from {slug})
asset.musicDrag: drag {file} onto the MUSIC track · click for info
asset.sfxDrag: drag {file} onto the SFX track · click for info
audio.sectionSubtitle: / per cue · drop SFX between cues
audio.continuousTitle: Row play continues to the next clip (off = one clip only)
audio.generateSfxTitle: Use the local LLM to read the script as a radio play and propose sound effects — favoring the kit you already have. Inserts them into the timeline on apply.
audio.voicesTitle: Assign a cloned voice to each speaker (writes manifest.voice.speaker_map).
audio.createVoiceTitle: Clone a voice: upload a short clean clip (mp3/wav/m4a/mp4) and OmniVoice creates a profile you can assign to a character.
audio.preCacheTitle: Load all of this episode's audio into the browser so playback is instant over the Cloudflare proxy
audio.selectCueHint: Select a cue on the left to see its full metadata.
audio.holdPlaceholder: (hold cue — typing here adds spoken VO)
createvoice.title: CREATE VOICE
createvoice.hintVoiceName: (e.g. Vivian — becomes the OmniVoice profile name)
createvoice.hintRefClip: (mp3 / wav / m4a / mp4 — a few clean seconds of the target voice)
createvoice.hintTestLine: (optional — generates a sample so you can hear the clone)
createvoice.placeholderTestLine: Leave blank for a default test line.
createvoice.workingMsg: Starting OmniVoice (first clone of the session can take up to ~3 min to warm the GPU) and cloning… keep this open.
createvoice.footerNote: Clones via OmniVoice on the 2080 Ti. The clip is auto-normalized to 24kHz mono. A same-named voice is replaced.
createvoice.doneFooterNote: Add it to a character in this show's Voice Roster / the manifest to use it for VO.
diagnostics.title: System diagnostics
diagnostics.bannerFailed: Some required checks failed — see below.
diagnostics.preflightNote: This runs the installer preflight (<0>{cmd}</0>) on the host. It only checks — it never installs anything.
docs.sharedEditorTitle: Editing this changes it for every show
docs.editorHint: UTF-8 · markdown · LF · Ctrl/Cmd+S to save
dopesheet.title: DOPE SHEET
dopesheet.dragHint: / drag a card from the timeline drawer onto a cue
dopesheet.continuousTitle: Row play continues to the next clip (off = one clip only)
toast.graphicImported: imported {asset} from {slug} — render it on the Graphics tab
filemenu.folderBody: Studio couldn't open a file manager on the machine it's running on — you may be browsing from another computer, or the server is headless. The show's files live at this path on the Studio machine:
filemenu.folderWindowsHint: From Windows, paste this into File Explorer:
filemenu.cloneVoicesBody.one: This import brought {n} voice as a reference clip. Re-cloning rebuilds it in this machine's OmniVoice and re-points {show}'s speakers at it. It starts OmniVoice and uses the GPU (~a few seconds per voice). You can do it later from the voice picker.
filemenu.cloneVoicesBody.other: This import brought {n} voices as reference clips. Re-cloning rebuilds them in this machine's OmniVoice and re-points {show}'s speakers at them. It starts OmniVoice and uses the GPU (~a few seconds per voice). You can do it later from the voice picker.
filemenu.cloneVoicesFailed: Failed: {names}. Re-run from the voice picker once OmniVoice is healthy.
filemenu.shutdownBody: This frees the GPU — it stops the ComfyUI, OmniVoice, and Ollama containers — and then stops the Studio server. To use Studio again you’ll need to start it from a terminal (<0>{cmd}</0>, or the systemd service).
filemenu.shuttingDown: MACU Studio is freeing the GPU and shutting down…
filemenu.shuttingDownBody: You can close this browser tab now. Start Studio again from a terminal when you want to come back.
filemenu.newShowHint: Episodes will live in a new folder. Voice/render defaults are copied from the current default show — edit them under Settings → Show metadata.
filemenu.newEpisodeHint: Scaffolds a manifest + script seeded from this show’s defaults. Then write the script and click Generate manifest.
filemenu.exportBody: Bundles the show’s text (script, manifest) + the title-card templates. With assets on, it also bundles the binaries the manifest references — OmniVoice voice reference clips, SFX, music, the character library (reference stills + takes), each episode’s stills, and the paid Higgsfield cloud clips. Stills and cloud clips carry their cache files, so importing them never re-bills. Local renders (zeroscope clips, final mp4) are never included — move those via a fresh render.
filemenu.exportAssetsCheckbox: Include assets (voices, SFX, music) — bigger zip
filemenu.exportVoiceHint: (A single voice can be exported from the Audio page’s voice picker.)
toast.copyFailed: copy failed — select the path and copy it manually
graphics.cardBlurb.macuTitle: Recurring show-open (intro layout). Wordmark is locked to “THE MACU / REPORT”; AI writes only the kicker tease + a station-ID sub.
graphics.cardBlurb.freshTitle: Per-episode segment title (intro layout). AI writes the two big title lines (short, all-caps, ominous) + kicker + a logline from the script.
graphics.cardBlurb.weather: Forecast segment (weather layout). A deadpan post-apocalyptic weather report.
toast.cardTextWritten: card text written — review + edit before render
toast.compCreated: card “{composition}” created ({count} fields) — fill the values, then Create + render
graphics.regenBtnTitle: Re-render this card from its saved layout + fields
graphics.editBtnTitle: Edit this card’s layout or text, then re-render
graphics.configureBtnTitle: This card has no saved layout/text yet — set them up once, then it becomes Regen-able
graphics.bespokeHint: Shows “Configure” (not “Regen”) because this card was authored as a description, not from an editable layout. Configure it once to make it Regen-able.
graphics.noTitleSelected: No title selected — pick one from the cards on the right.
graphics.cardRegenTitle: Regen — re-render from saved layout + fields
graphics.cardConfigureTitle: Configure — this card has no editable layout yet; set it up to make it regen-able
graphics.thumbClickTitle: Click to enlarge · browse versions · see the template + fields
graphics.editModalFooterHint: Saves your changes and re-renders the card.
graphics.newModalFooterHint: Saves the card and renders it as an mp4.
graphics.createPendingBriefTitle: You wrote a brief in step 2 — click ‘Generate composition’ first, or clear the brief to use the selected layout
graphics.step1Hint: A short id, used as the filename. e.g. lower_third, scoreboard.
graphics.cantRenameHint: — can’t rename; make a new card to change the id
graphics.step2Hint: A “composition” is the animated template — it decides where the text sits and how it moves. The preview shows the chosen layout; ‹TOKENS› mark where your step-3 text lands.
graphics.bespokeCardDesc: this one was authored as a description, not from a saved layout, so there are no editable fields yet. Pick a layout below + fill the text to make it editable (or design a new layout with AI).
graphics.designWithAiSummary: ✨ or design a brand-new layout with AI
graphics.designWithAiHelp: Only needed when no layout above fits. Describe the card; the local model (Qwen) writes a new HyperFrames layout and saves it under the name from step 1. You’ll still fill the text in step 3.
graphics.briefPlaceholder: e.g. a Crater Bowl scoreboard: SECTOR NINE SLAGS vs SECTOR FOUR GLOWBOYS, big score numbers ticking 2 → 1, ‘PLAYERS REMAINING’ underneath
graphics.genCompBtnTitle: Generate a brand-new HyperFrames layout from this brief, saved under the name in step 1
graphics.nameFirstWarning: ↑ name the card in step 1 first — it becomes the new layout’s name.
graphics.generateFirstWarning: Click “Generate composition” to build this layout. Until you do, “Create + render” uses the layout selected above and ignores this brief.
graphics.compGeneratedSuccess: layout “{key}” created and selected above — now fill the text in step 3.
graphics.step3Hint: These values replace the ‹TOKENS› in the layout. Type them, or let the AI draft them from this episode’s script.
graphics.writeTextBtnTitle: Drafts the text below from this episode’s script, in this card type’s voice
graphics.cardTypeNote: Card type only matters if you click “Write text with AI” — it picks the voice, which fields get written, and snaps the layout to that card type’s composition. Typing the fields yourself ignores it.
graphics.thumbModalDesc: Renders the youtube_thumb composition to final/{slug}_thumb.png. The current thumbnail is archived first.
graphics.writeThumbBtnTitle: Write a punchy deadpan thumbnail hook with the local LLM (Ollama)
log.title: ACTIVITY LOG
log.empty: No activity yet. Toasts and notifications will collect here.
metadata.title: METADATA
metadata.emptyHint: Select a clip on the timeline or an asset in the drawer.
metadata.libDragHint: Drag onto the {track} track to place it.
placeholder.comingSoon: Coming soon. v0.1 ships Stage 5 (Assembly) end-to-end. Other stages will land in subsequent versions.
script.modeDiffTitle: Compare synced versions (green added / red cut)
script.generateTitle: Re-parse script.md into manifest cues (preview before writing)
script.mergeHelp: Cues are re-parsed from <0>script.md</0> and merged in; voice profiles, character seeds, b-roll, comfyui/style/music and title assets are preserved. A timestamped <1>manifest.json.bak</1> is written before applying.
script.unmappedHint: Add them to voice.speaker_map (Manifest drawer) or those cues won’t get VO.
script.noChangesDetail: Script matches the manifest — nothing to write.
script.noVersionsYet: No synced versions yet. Hit the git sync button to snapshot this script, then come back to compare.
script.oneVersionOnly: Only one version so far ({label}). Sync again after your next edit to get a diff.
script.noDiffChanges: No changes between these two versions.
sfx.delayTitle: delay nudge — seconds, signed (− earlier / + later from the anchor cue)
sfx.phMusicPrompt: e.g. 'sickly lo-fi big-band waltz, tape hiss, mono'
sfx.phAgenPrompt: e.g. 'heavy iron door slam, dry, close mic'
sfx.helpAgen: Local AudioGen → 24 kHz/−3 dBFS into the kit · pinned to
sfx.helpThenDrag: then drag it from the library
toast.gpuBusy: GPU busy — a render is active. Try again when idle.
sfxgen.title: GENERATE SFX LIST
sfxgen.idle.body: Read the script as a radio play and propose sound effects, favoring your existing kit.
sfxgen.idle.hint: Press Begin to start — spins up Ollama on the GPU, ~30-60s.
sfxgen.loading.body: Asking the local LLM (Qwen2.5-7B) to read the script as a radio play…
sfxgen.error.gpuBusy: GPU is busy — a render is active. Try again when it's idle.
sfxgen.review.empty: No sound-effect opportunities found — the model judged the script needs none, or everything is already placed.
sfxgen.section.existing: USING EXISTING SFX ({count}) — already in the kit
sfxgen.section.acquire: NEEDS ACQUIRING ({count}) — edit the search, fetch later from the library
sfxgen.review.helpText: Apply inserts these into the audio timeline (manifest.sfx[]) exactly like a drag-drop — per-gap delays are auto-staggered. Library effects play immediately; "to acquire" effects are placeholders until you fetch them (Library → Add → Freesound) under the shown filename; a render skips any sound not yet on disk.
sfxgen.gain.title: gain — 0–1 linear
sfxgen.gain.label: g
sfxgen.toast.applied: SFX list applied — {placed} placed ({reused} from library{acq})
shotgen.title: GENERATE SHOT LIST
shotgen.onlyMissingTooltip: On: only plan cues that have no shots yet (won't touch cues you've already set). Off: re-plan every cue (overwrites existing shots).
shotgen.loadingMain: Asking the local LLM (Qwen2.5-7B) to plan the shots…
shotgen.errorGpuBusy: GPU is busy — a render is active. Try again when it's idle.
shotgen.emptyAllFilled: Every cue already has shots — nothing to fill. Uncheck the box above to re-plan the whole episode.
shotgen.emptyNoShots: The model returned no shots. Try again.
shotgen.sectionNewChars: NEW characters — minted (edit the core if you like)
shotgen.applyFootnote: Apply overwrites each planned cue's shots[] and writes new character/b-roll defs. Reused keys keep their existing cores + seeds.
toast.shotApplied: shot list applied — {appliedCues} cues, {newChars} new chars, {newBroll} new broll
sysstat.outerTooltip: host CPU · GPU · GPU RAM · storage disk I/O (updates ~2s)
terminal.title: TERMINAL
terminal.helpMain: Detached. Click Connect to attach to the <0>{session}</0> tmux session and talk to Claude directly.
thumb.title: YOUTUBE THUMBNAIL
thumb.promoteTip: Make this version the live thumbnail
thumb.browseToOlderTip: Browse to an older version to select it
thumb.fieldsNotRecorded: — not recorded (rendered before metadata tracking, or no fields)
timeline.previewSubtitle: / rendered output · click a cue to play · audio/graphic edits show after a re-render
timeline.noVideoYet: No rendered video yet — run the episode (Assembly → Run) to enable the synced preview.
timeline.timelineSubtitle: / drag assets from the drawer onto a track · scrub on the ruler · drag clips to move, edges to trim
timeline.emptyVo: VO appears once cues have audio
timeline.shotMoveTip: drag to move/reorder · drop on the drawer to remove
topbar.syncDotPending: Pending changes — not synced to git
topbar.stopConfirm: EMERGENCY STOP — kill the active render, clear the ComfyUI queue, and free GPU memory?
topbar.stopTitle: Emergency stop: kill the active render, clear the ComfyUI queue, and free GPU memory
topbar.gitSyncTitle: Sync script + manifest + youtube.txt to git
topbar.updateTitle: Update available — {behind} new commit(s). Click to review and install.
toast.emergencyStopStart: emergency stop — killing render + freeing GPU…
toast.emergencyStopDone: emergency stop complete — render killed, queue cleared, GPU freed
update.title: Software update
update.phaseBuilding: Rebuilding (deps + UI)… this can take a minute.
update.phaseRestarting: Restarting — the page will reload automatically.
update.phaseRestartNeeded: Update built. Restart the server manually to load it.
update.dirtyWarning: This checkout has uncommitted local changes — auto-update is disabled to avoid clobbering them. Commit or discard them (or update from a terminal) first.
update.updateHint: "Update & restart" pulls, rebuilds the UI, and relaunches the service (~1 min). The page reloads itself when it's back.
update.setupIntro: The built-in updater can't finish this one on its own (it can't use sudo). From the macu-pipeline folder, run the command(s) below — ./deploy/install.sh is safe to re-run and pulls the new code too:
update.noticeIntro: You can update with one click. Just so you know:
update.idleHint: Click "Check for updates" to look for a newer build.
update.gpuBusyBody: It looks like your GPU is busy ({free} MiB free) — a render may be active. Updating rebuilds and restarts Studio, which can interrupt that work. Are you sure you want to update and restart now?
video.generateShotListTitle: Use the local LLM to propose a shot list from the script (reuses recurring characters)
video.precacheTitle: Load all rendered shot masters into the browser so previews are instant over the Cloudflare proxy
video.seedVersionReadOnly: Seed of the version being viewed (read-only)
video.emptyManifest: No characters or b-roll in manifest.
video.addShotHelp: Adds a {kind} key to the manifest. Render it from the SHOT LIST.
video.addShotHelpWithCue: Adds a {kind} key to the manifest and a shot to @{cue}. Render it from the SHOT LIST.
video.cloudChipTitle: Higgsfield cloud shots: count, estimated credits for non-cached shots, and your balance. Crop/trim edits never re-bill.
video.cloudChipIdleTitle: Higgsfield is connected. Add a cloud shot via + Add Shot (kind: higgsfield or lipsync); the estimated cost will appear here.
video.cloudChipDisconnectedTitle: Higgsfield cloud video generation — connect your account in Settings → Higgsfield to use it.
video.kindLipsync: Lipsync shot (audio-driven by the cue's VO)
video.cloudNoSeed: Cloud models don't take a seed
video.cloudNeedsCue: Cloud shots must be attached to a cue
video.addShotHelpCloud: Generates this shot in the cloud via Higgsfield (billed per generation; see the ☁ estimate). Leave model empty for the episode default. A source still makes it image-to-video.
video.addShotHelpLipsync: Replaces the cue's shots with ONE lipsync shot: the character still is animated to speak the cue's VO. Needs a source still; VO longer than ~12s is chunked and chained automatically.
timeline.lipsyncSolo: Cue {cue} has a lipsync shot — it must stay the only shot in its cue (the clip spans the whole VO).
timeline.shotCloudTip: click to edit crop/trim · drag to move
metadata.shotEditHint: Crop, trim, and jank are applied at assembly from the cached clip — editing them re-assembles without re-billing Higgsfield credits.
assembly.costError: Estimate unavailable: {msg}. You can still render — non-cached cloud shots will bill your account.
assembly.costInsufficient: Estimated cost exceeds your credit balance. The render may fail partway (each finished shot stays cached).
assembly.costUnknowns: {n} shot(s) have unknown cost (see notes above) — the total is a lower bound.
toast.noMastersToCache: No rendered masters to cache yet
voicepicker.castVoicesSubtitle: — assign a cloned voice per character
voicepicker.omnivoiceStopped: OmniVoice stopped — clone or render to list voices
voicepicker.noCharacters: No characters yet — add one below, or Generate manifest to pull them from the script.
voicepicker.exportVoiceTitle: Download {name}'s reference clip — re-importable on another machine
youtube.selectScript: Select a script on the left.
youtube.notConfiguredBody: Add an API key + channel id to <0>~/.config/macu-studio/youtube.json</0> (or set <1>YOUTUBE_API_KEY</1> / <2>YOUTUBE_CHANNEL_ID</2>) to show matched uploads here.
youtube.captions.title: Captions
youtube.captions.clientHelp: One-time: create an OAuth client of type "TVs and Limited Input devices" in your Google Cloud project (the one with the YouTube Data API enabled), then paste its ID + secret here.
youtube.captions.noVideo: No matched YouTube video for this episode.
youtube.captions.noTracks: No subtitle tracks yet — localize this episode first.
youtube.captions.quotaNote: Each upload uses ~400 of YouTube's daily quota (~25 uploads/day on the default limit).
filemenu.macuWebConnectHint: On mayorawesome.com, open your show → Manage → Connect MACU Studio → Generate connect token, copy it, and paste it here. It wires the remote and push credentials in one step.
filemenu.macuWebConnectedHint: Connected to <0>{base}</0>. Checked episodes go public; unchecked are pushed but hidden as drafts. Then Publish.
toast.macuWebCommitted: committed locally ({files} files) — set creds to push
publish.thumbNone: No rendered thumbnail yet — run the render (stage 5) first.
publish.videoHint: MACU Web doesn’t host video — paste the link to where it lives (YouTube). It embeds on the episode page.
publish.ytNoMatch: No channel match for this episode title.
publish.connectHint: Paste your connect token (Manage page on mayorawesome.com → Connect MACU Studio).
publish.publishHint: Edit the fields above, then publish to push them to the site.
script.styleGuideTitle: How the manifest parser reads your script — opens the Docs tab
wizard.serviceDown: A service this step needs isn't running right now. You can skip it and come back later — your progress is saved.
wizard.bannerHint: New to Studio? Take a short guided walkthrough that builds a real episode.
wizard.welcome.title: Let's make your first episode
wizard.welcome.body: This walkthrough builds a real episode end to end — script, voices, and a final video — in about fifteen minutes. It starts by creating a small practice episode in your current show. Nothing here touches your other work.
wizard.script.title: 1 · Write the script
wizard.script.body: We seeded a tiny starter script. Every line like **NAME:** becomes a spoken cue, every ## heading starts a segment, and a » line is a shot direction. Read it, change a line or two, or write your own — then press Generate Manifest (top right) and Apply.
wizard.cast.title: 2 · Cast the voices
wizard.cast.body: Each speaker needs a voice. The starter leaves NARRATOR and GUEST unassigned — pick a voice for each using the voice picker beside their name. (You can skip this: unassigned speakers fall back to the default robot voice.)
wizard.renderVo.title: 3 · Render the voiceover
wizard.renderVo.body: Now turn the lines into audio. Use “Regen missing” to render every cue at once, or the regen button on a single cue, then press play to hear it. The counter tracks how many are done.
wizard.sfx.title: 4 · Sound effects & music (optional)
wizard.sfx.body: Add a little texture: generate sound-effect suggestions to drop between cues, or enable a music bed. This step is optional — skip it any time and move on.
wizard.sfx.goal: At least one sound effect placed
wizard.graphics.title: 5 · Title cards (optional)
wizard.graphics.body: Title cards and lower-thirds are built here from HyperFrames compositions. Render one to see how it looks. Optional — skip if you'd rather keep it simple.
wizard.shots.title: 6 · Video shots (optional)
wizard.shots.body: The video stage turns your shot directions into short clips with ComfyUI. Use “Generate shots” to propose a shot list, then render one. This needs the GPU services — skip it if they aren't running.
wizard.assemble.title: 7 · Assemble the episode
wizard.assemble.body: Assembly runs the full pipeline — voice, video, music, subtitles — and stitches the final video. Press Run and watch the live log. This is the GPU-heavy step; skip it if the services are down and come back later.
wizard.publish.title: 8 · Publishing (a tour stop)
wizard.publish.body: When an episode is ready you can publish it to your MACU Web site and link a YouTube video here. We won't publish anything during the walkthrough — this is just so you know where it lives. Have a look around, then continue.
wizard.done.title: That's the whole loop!
wizard.done.body: You took a script all the way to a finished episode. From here: edit the practice episode freely, start a real one from the menu, or open Docs for the deeper reference. You can replay this walkthrough any time from the menu.
tour.theme.title: Make it yours
tour.theme.body: Settings → Theme (in this menu) restyles the whole studio: the amber terminal with five accent colors, plus full looks — Starship, Wasteland, Slate Pro, Movie Palace, Dracula, and Pretty Princess. Switching is instant and remembered.
wizard.theme.title: Pick your look
wizard.theme.body: Before we build anything, make the room yours. Open the project menu (top-left) → Settings → Theme. Click a color dot on the Terminal row for a quick accent change, or try a full theme like Starship or Movie Palace. It applies instantly and is saved in this browser.
wizard.theme.goal: Pick any theme — or keep the amber terminal and move on.
filemenu.archiveEpisodeBody: Archive <0>{slug}</0>? This moves the episode's files out of the active list into the archive folder on disk. You can restore it later from Settings → Archive.
filemenu.archiveShowBody: Archive <0>{show}</0> and all its episodes? This moves the whole show out of the active list into the archive folder on disk. You can restore it later from Settings → Archive.
settings.archive.title: Archived episodes & shows
settings.archive.help: Archived items are physically moved out of the active lists into an archive folder on disk. Restore one to bring it back.
settings.archive.slugTaken: Slug {slug} is taken by a live episode — pick a new one
tour.characters.title: Character library
tour.characters.body: Your show's reusable cast: craft reference stills here (locally via ComfyUI, on Higgsfield, or a remote render box), then pull characters into episodes for image-to-video and lipsync shots.
characters.empty: No characters yet — create one, or import from an episode below.
characters.fieldCorePh: used by video shots when a shot has no prompt of its own
characters.fieldStillPromptPh: portrait of a 1970s news anchor at a broadcast desk… (drives Generate below)
characters.noTakes: No takes yet — set a still prompt and hit Generate.
characters.genPromptPh: leave empty to use the still prompt
characters.useHelp: Copies this take into the episode's stills/ and links the character into its manifest. The still is stamped fresh, so nothing re-bills.
characters.useConfirm: This episode already has a different still for this character.
characters.useInvalidates: Replacing it marks {n} already-paid cloud shot(s) stale — they will RE-BILL on the next render: {shots}
characters.importHelp: Seeds the library from an episode's manifest characters (prompts + existing stills become take-001). Characters already in the library are skipped.
video.stillNone: No stills yet — create characters and generate takes on the Characters tab.
engines.claude.title: Claude Code
engines.claude.help: Connect an agent to this Studio's MCP server, or route the script tools (shot lists, SFX plans, card text) through your Claude subscription — no local model needed.
engines.claude.notInstalled: not found on this machine — claude.com/claude-code
engines.claude.textgenHint: Tip: set Script tools above to "Claude Code" to generate shot lists with your Claude subscription instead of the local Ollama model — handy on light installs, and it skips the GPU entirely.
sync.title: Sync — {show}
sync.help: Reconciles this show's working text (scripts, manifests, docs, character records) with your other Studio installs through your macu-web connection. Binaries (takes, stills, clips, voices) travel via Import/Export in the menu.
sync.pullTitle: Will pull (newer on the other Studio)
sync.conflictHelp: The losing version of any conflicted file is kept locally as a timestamped .bak next to the original.
sync.notConnected: Not connected to macu-web. Connect on the Publish page — both Studios must use the same connection key — then sync.
